settings.title: Executive Portraits for Marlow and Finch
settings.summary: We made a set of natural, on-brand portraits for a law firm's whole team in a single day.
settings.description:
Marlow and Finch had a wall of mismatched headshots taken over a decade and wanted one consistent set for their site and bios. Different photographers, different backgrounds, and different lighting meant the team page looked assembled from a dozen unrelated sources rather than one firm.

The brief

The firm wanted portraits that looked natural and approachable, not stiff corporate headshots, and they wanted the whole team to match. They also could not spare people for long, so the entire staff had to be photographed in a single day without disrupting the office.

How we did it

We brought a portable studio to their office and set it up in a spare room, which let people step out for a few minutes between meetings rather than travel anywhere. We photographed 22 staff in one day, keeping the light, the background, and the framing identical for everyone so the final set would line up.

  • An on-site portable studio set up in the office
  • 22 staff portraits in a single day
  • Consistent lighting, background, and crop across the set
  • Files prepared for both the website and printed bios

Holding 22 portraits to one look across a full day was the discipline of the job. We locked the lighting and the camera position at the start and left them fixed, so the last portrait of the afternoon matches the first of the morning.

The result

The firm now has a clean, current set of portraits that all match across the site and the bios. Onboarding a new hire means a quick add in the same setup rather than a full reshoot, so the team page stays consistent as people come and go.
